name: Flemish Beef Stew
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch salt pork in boiling water for 5 minutes. Drain and rinse well; pat dry. Transfer to a heavy large skillet and cook over medium flame until lightly browned. Remove with a slotted spoon and reserve for use in salads or other dishes.
2. Increase heat to medium high. Add beef to the skillet in batches (do not crowd) and cook until well browned on all sides, turning with a spatula. Transfer meat to a heavy 2 to 3 quart saucepan.
3. Add onions to the skillet and reduce heat slightly. Cook until deep golden brown about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. Blend in flour and cook for about 30 seconds, watching carefully so flour does not burn.
5. Add stout and stir, scraping up any browned bits. Bring mixture to a boil.
6. Pour over beef. Blend in vinegar, sugar, parsley, bay leaf, and thyme. Cover saucepan and simmer the mixture for 30 minutes.
7. Spread mustard over bread. Press bread into the stew. Cover and cook until meat is tender, about 1 hour. Remove and discard the bay leaf. Serve immediately with boiled potatoes, green salad, and beer.
